turns.content:
Here's the full inventory. I sorted every reference-emitting target into three buckets by *what kind* of edit each needs — because "mention the policy" means something different for a target that already duplicates the rule versus one that's silent.

## Bucket A — Restate their own `ticket.toml` link form (should *defer* to the global switch, not re-specify it)

These are the scattered duplicates — the exact anti-pattern the AGENTS.md redesign just eliminated at the top level, still living copy-pasted across prompts. Each hardcodes `[<short-id> <title>](…/ticket.toml)`, which means every future format change is an N-file manual sync. Convert these to "defer to the Clickable Reference Policy in AGENTS.md."

| Target | Evidence |
|---|---|
| ticket.prompt.md | L35, L42 — full `[<short-id> <title>](…/ticket.toml)` form restated |
| tickets.prompt.md | L36, L43 — same form, plural |
| spec.prompt.md | L27, L35 — same form for related tickets |
| ticket-system.instructions.md | L444, L445, L446 — the most verbose restatement of the whole render rule |
| spec-system.instructions.md | L74, L171 — "link exact ticket folder paths" (no render form, but same domain) |
| spec.agent.md | L43 — "exact related ticket folder paths returned by ticket tools" |

## Bucket B — Reference-heavy output, **zero** formatting guidance (silent offenders)

These emit lists of tickets/specs/code in their response contract but say nothing about link form — exactly the targets you named. They currently rely on the agent *remembering* AGENTS.md. A one-line explicit pointer closes that gap.

| Target | Evidence |
|---|---|
| ticket-next.prompt.md | Response section L32-35 lists "the chosen ticket" — no link form anywhere in file |
| ticket-refinement.agent.md | Whole file creates/reviews/splits tickets; output section has no reference-format rule |
| interview.agent.md | L46 "recommended ticket/spec update", L37 "exact ticket/spec follow-up" — no link form |
| reviews.prompt.md | Reviews many `in-review` tickets + specs + audits + referenced code (L48-53); no render rule |

## Bucket C — Already say "forward-slash markdown links" but don't point at the canonical policy

Half-aligned — they enforce *some* of the normalization rules inline but don't reference the single source, so they'll drift when the policy changes (e.g. if you flip the default mode to `viewer`).

| Target | Evidence |
|---|---|
| handoff.prompt.md | L50, L54, L55 — "markdown links with forward slashes only", entities as links |
| handoff-tickets.prompt.md | L50, L52, L53 — same, ticket-scoped |

## Recommendation

The highest-leverage move mirrors the fix that just landed in AGENTS.md: **don't restate, defer.** For Bucket A, replace each inline `ticket.toml` render spec with a single line — "Render all entity references per the Clickable Reference Policy in AGENTS.md" — plus the one genuinely local rule worth keeping (never synthesize a path; fetch the authoritative folder path from the API first). For Buckets B and C, add that same one-line pointer to each Response/Output section.

That collapses ~15 scattered restatements down to one canonical definition with pointers — the same "single switch" property you built globally, now honored by the leaf targets instead of quietly contradicting it.

Note: this is the recon deliverable — I'm read-only, so I gathered and cited but did not edit. If you want, hand this to the rule/prompt-editing agent and it can execute Bucket A → C in that priority order, since Bucket A is where the real duplication debt lives.

I’m going to apply this inventory directly: first I’ll locate each target file and current wording, then patch Bucket A to replace hardcoded render rules with a canonical pointer, and finally add the same pointer to Buckets B and C where guidance is missing or partial.I’m collecting the exact file paths and current line text for each bucket so the edits are precise and minimal.
I found all 12 target files and will quickly load the two path-scoped instruction files that govern edits in prompts/agents/instructions before patching.

I confirmed the path-scoped rules and next I’m verifying each target’s actual source of truth; if a file is generated, I’ll patch its corresponding .rule body instead of editing generated output directly.
I confirmed most targets are generated; I’m now switching to their canonical .rule bodies so the change is durable and survives regeneration.
I’ve located all source rule entries; next I’m editing those bodies (plus the one non-generated agent file) so a single regen updates every generated target cleanly.
I’ve mapped the exact duplicated text; now I’m loading the remaining rule bodies so I can apply one consistent defer-to-policy line across Buckets B and C too.

I’ve got the exact text edits queued: Bucket A will drop hardcoded ticket-link rendering, and Buckets B/C will get an explicit canonical-policy pointer while preserving the local “never synthesize path” rule. I’m applying those in the .rule sources now.

arguments.explanation: Update rule source bodies and one direct agent file to defer reference rendering to AGENTS.md Clickable Reference Policy while keeping authoritative path requirements.
arguments.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: c:\Users\linus\git\graph_app\context-engine\.rule\rules\62f3b15f-ed6f-4093-b218-6ec3cb4fcaa6\body.md
@@
-11. Extract the exact canonical ticket folder path from ticket-api output. If the first create or match response does not include the folder path, run an immediate follow-up ticket-api command that returns the authoritative path before composing the chat response or updating the spec. Use that exact returned folder path as the link base and append `/ticket.toml` only when rendering the markdown target.
-12. Ensure the spec records the request's requirements or goals before implementation begins and links the ticket with a target of the form `<exact ticket folder path>/ticket.toml` when available.
+11. Extract the exact canonical ticket folder path from ticket-api output. If the first create or match response does not include the folder path, run an immediate follow-up ticket-api command that returns the authoritative path before composing the chat response or updating the spec.
+12. Ensure the spec records the request's requirements or goals before implementation begins and renders ticket references per the Clickable Reference Policy in `AGENTS.md`.
@@
-- created or matched ticket folder path and title, rendered as a markdown link of the form `[<short-id> <title>](<exact ticket folder path returned by ticket-api output>/ticket.toml)`
+- created or matched ticket folder path and title, rendered as a markdown link per the Clickable Reference Policy in `AGENTS.md`
*** End Patch

turns.content:
Implemented exactly as requested: Bucket A now defers to the canonical policy instead of restating the ticket.toml render form, and Buckets B/C now include an explicit pointer to the canonical policy.

What I changed

1. Bucket A (remove duplicated render spec, keep authoritative-path rule)
- Updated [ticket.prompt.md](.agents/prompts/ticket.prompt.md#L36) and [ticket.prompt.md](.agents/prompts/ticket.prompt.md#L42)
- Updated [tickets.prompt.md](.agents/prompts/tickets.prompt.md#L37) and [tickets.prompt.md](.agents/prompts/tickets.prompt.md#L43)
- Updated [spec.prompt.md](.agents/prompts/spec.prompt.md#L27) and [spec.prompt.md](.agents/prompts/spec.prompt.md#L35)
- Updated [ticket-system.instructions.md](.agents/instructions/ticket-system.instructions.md#L446)
- Updated [spec-system.instructions.md](.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md#L74) and [spec-system.instructions.md](.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md#L172)
- Updated [spec.agent.md](.agents/agents/spec.agent.md#L43)

2. Bucket B (silent offenders: add explicit canonical pointer)
- Updated [ticket-next.prompt.md](.agents/prompts/ticket-next.prompt.md#L36)
- Updated [ticket-refinement.agent.md](.agents/agents/ticket-refinement.agent.md#L59)
- Updated [interview.agent.md](.agents/agents/interview.agent.md#L47)
- Updated [reviews.prompt.md](.agents/prompts/reviews.prompt.md#L91)

3. Bucket C (keep local normalization guidance, also add canonical pointer)
- Updated [handoff.prompt.md](.agents/prompts/handoff.prompt.md#L51)
- Updated [handoff-tickets.prompt.md](.agents/prompts/handoff-tickets.prompt.md#L51)

Source-of-truth handling

- For generated targets, I edited the canonical rule bodies under [.rule/rules](.rule/rules) and regenerated outputs.
- Global sync command failed in this workspace with a pre-existing path/storage error, so I regenerated the affected targets individually with rule generate-file/generate-target, which succeeded for all edited generated files.

Notes

- I did not touch unrelated session artifacts under .session.
- If you want, I can also run a focused check to identify why full sync-targets currently fails in this workspace so future global regenerations are clean.
